text.title: Rice For Design Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Rice For Design. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Reach = affected user count in the next 30 days (not total users).
rule.rationale: Reach = affected user count in the next 30 days (not total users).

rule.statement: Impact uses a 3-point scale (low=0.5, medium=1, high=2) tied to usability lift evidence.
rule.rationale: Impact uses a 3-point scale (low=0.5, medium=1, high=2) tied to usability lift evidence.

rule.statement: Confidence is bound by evidence: high needs ≥3 corroborating sources.
rule.rationale: Confidence is bound by evidence: high needs ≥3 corroborating sources.

rule.statement: Every scored item ties back to a friction-map entry; orphan items rejected.
rule.rationale: Every scored item ties back to a friction-map entry; orphan items rejected.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-rice-for-design.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
